intro: Candace was a dynastic title, not a personal name, borne by the queens of the ancient Nubian kingdom of Meroe (Kush), situated south of Egypt along the Nile. The sole biblical reference occurs in Acts 8:27, where a high-ranking court official — the treasurer — of "Candace, queen of the Ethiopians" is encountered by Philip the evangelist on the road from Jerusalem to Gaza. This eunuch had been to Jerusalem to worship and was reading Isaiah 53 in his chariot when Philip came alongside him, explained the passage as fulfilled in Jesus, and baptized him. The narrative is among the first explicit accounts of the gospel reaching sub-Saharan Africa and an important moment in the spread of Christianity to Gentiles.
